log:
# Change log — Fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems

## 2026-06-27

Ingested source: Case et al. 2025, "Fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems", *Nature* 638,
49-57 (DOI:10.1038/s41586-024-08419-4). Saved immutable bytes to `raw/source.md`.

- Created the Publication source node "Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review"
  with `raw_source: [raw/source.md]`. The converted source contains the abstract, three
  figure captions and the full 200+ reference list (body text paywalled); entity/claim
  extraction draws on those.
- Authored 30 typed concept docs:
  - Organism (8): Fungal kingdom, Candida auris, Aspergillus fumigatus, Cryptococcus gattii,
    Batrachochytrium dendrobatidis, Pseudogymnoascus destructans, Coccidioides,
    Magnaporthe oryzae.
  - Disease (7): Candidiasis, Invasive aspergillosis, Cryptococcosis, Coccidioidomycosis,
    White-nose syndrome, Chytridiomycosis, Wheat blast.
  - Molecule (5): Amphotericin B, Fluconazole, Penicillin, Psilocybin, Fungal melanin.
  - MolecularClass (1): Azole antifungals.
  - Exposure (1): Climate change.
  - BiologicalPathway (2): Carbon sequestration, Nutrient cycling.
  - Phenotype (5): Antifungal drug resistance, Bat population decline, Amphibian population
    decline, Crop yield loss, Ionizing radiation tolerance.
  - GeographicLocation (1): Western United States.
- Added 71 provenance-stamped edges (causes, member_of, treats, predisposes_to,
  associated_with, participates_in, has_phenotype, derives_from, located_in,
  affects_response_to, plus a reported_in back-link on every node to the source).
- Lint: 31 nodes, 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 infos. All edge objects resolve to created nodes.
name: Fungal impacts on ecosystems

Amphibian population decline.body:
# Amphibian population decline

Amphibian population decline refers to worldwide losses of frog, toad and salamander
populations. The review attributes a large share of these declines and extinctions to
chytridiomycosis caused by *Batrachochytrium* fungi, describing it as among the most
destructive wildlife diseases driven by a pathogen.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Amphotericin B.body:
# Amphotericin B

Amphotericin B is a polyene antifungal that binds ergosterol in fungal membranes. The review
discusses it among the limited classes of antifungal drugs available to treat severe human
fungal infections (alongside azoles and echinocandins), and frames the narrowness of the
antifungal arsenal as a key vulnerability against emerging, resistant fungal pathogens.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Antifungal drug resistance.body:
# Antifungal drug resistance

Antifungal drug resistance is the ability of fungi to survive drug concentrations that would
normally inhibit or kill them. The review frames the worldwide emergence of antifungal
resistance — driven partly by agricultural azole use — as a threat to both human health and
food security, made more dangerous by the small number of antifungal drug classes (azoles,
polyenes, echinocandins) available. Emerging pathogens such as *Candida auris* are often
multidrug resistant.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Aspergillus fumigatus.body:
# Aspergillus fumigatus

*Aspergillus fumigatus* is a ubiquitous saprotrophic mould and the principal agent of
invasive aspergillosis in humans. The review notes the worldwide emergence of azole
resistance in *A. fumigatus*, driven in part by agricultural azole use, as a threat to both
human health and food security. Invasive mould infections also followed environmental
disruption such as Hurricane Harvey.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Azole antifungals.body:
# Azole antifungals

Azoles are the largest class of antifungal drugs and fungicides, inhibiting fungal ergosterol
biosynthesis. The review frames azoles as central to both human medicine and agriculture, and
identifies their dual use as a driver of the worldwide emergence of azole resistance in
pathogens such as *Aspergillus fumigatus* — a threat to human health and food security alike.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Bat population decline.body:
# Bat population decline

Bat population decline refers to the mass mortality of hibernating bats observed across North
America. The review attributes much of this decline to white-nose syndrome caused by
*Pseudogymnoascus destructans*, citing it as a leading example of a fungal pathogen reshaping
wildlife populations and ecosystems.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Batrachochytrium dendrobatidis.body:
# Batrachochytrium dendrobatidis

*Batrachochytrium dendrobatidis* (Bd) is a chytrid fungus that causes chytridiomycosis, a
disease responsible for catastrophic amphibian declines worldwide. The review uses Bd, and
its salamander-infecting relative *Batrachochytrium salamandrivorans*, as flagship examples
of fungal pathogens devastating wildlife — spread driven in part by human-mediated movement
of animals.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Candida auris.body:
# Candida auris

*Candida auris* is an emerging, frequently multidrug-resistant yeast pathogen of humans. It
is highlighted in the review as a paradigm of an emerging fungal threat whose spread and
azole/echinocandin resistance challenge human health. Its emergence has been linked to
climate-driven thermal adaptation, a recurring theme of the review.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Candidiasis.body:
# Candidiasis

Candidiasis is infection caused by *Candida* yeasts, including life-threatening invasive
disease (candidemia). The review discusses candidiasis — and emerging multidrug-resistant
agents such as *Candida auris* — as a major and growing human fungal disease burden.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Carbon sequestration.body:
# Carbon sequestration

Carbon sequestration is the capture and long-term storage of carbon in ecosystems. The
review emphasizes that fungi, as heterotrophs feeding on almost any organic carbon, sequester
carbon into Earth's ecosystems while recycling nutrients through the decay of dead plants and
animals — a foundational ecosystem service of the fungal kingdom.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecos.body:
# Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Review by Nicola T. Case, Sarah J. Gurr, Matthew C. Fisher and colleagues (a large
multi-author consortium), *Nature* **638**, 49-57 (2025), "Fungal impacts on Earth's
ecosystems". The review surveys the fungal kingdom's role in Earth's ecosystems: nutrient
cycling and carbon sequestration, human-directed applications (food, beverages, biofuels,
pharmaceuticals), and the rising threat of fungal infections to crops, wildlife and humans,
amplified by human/animal movement and climate change. It also examines opportunities to
harness fungi against these threats.

This node is the in-bundle source anchor; its immutable bytes are at `raw/source.md`.
Key reported figures: the fungal kingdom comprises more than two million species with over
95% still undescribed.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecos.identifier: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Chytridiomycosis.body:
# Chytridiomycosis

Chytridiomycosis is a skin disease of amphibians caused by the chytrid fungi
*Batrachochytrium dendrobatidis* and *B. salamandrivorans*. The review cites it as the
single most devastating wildlife disease attributable to a pathogen, driving global
amphibian population declines and extinctions, with spread accelerated by the international
movement of amphibians.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Climate change.body:
# Climate change

Climate change is the long-term shift in temperature and weather patterns driven by human
activity. The review identifies it as a key amplifier of fungal threats: warming may select
for thermotolerant fungi able to infect endothermic hosts, precipitation/heat/drought drive
coccidioidomycosis expansion, and shifting climate disrupts fungal interactions in plants,
wildlife and humans (Fig. 1). It is also projected to substantially reduce ectomycorrhizal
fungal diversity in North American forests.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Coccidioides.body:
# Coccidioides

*Coccidioides* (species *C. immitis* and *C. posadasii*) is a dimorphic soil fungus endemic
to arid regions of the western USA and the Americas, and the cause of coccidioidomycosis
(Valley fever). The review highlights it as a climate-sensitive pathogen: precipitation,
heat and drought drive the incidence and geographic expansion of coccidioidomycosis, and it
has even been reported in southern sea otters, underscoring fungal threats to wildlife.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Coccidioidomycosis.body:
# Coccidioidomycosis

Coccidioidomycosis (Valley fever) is the disease caused by inhaling *Coccidioides* spores,
endemic to the arid western United States. The review highlights it as a climate-sensitive
fungal disease: a longitudinal surveillance study found that precipitation, heat and drought
drive the incidence and the geographic expansion of coccidioidomycosis, projecting further
spread under climate change.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Crop yield loss.body:
# Crop yield loss

Crop yield loss is the reduction in agricultural harvest caused by disease, pests or abiotic
stress. The review highlights fungal crop pathogens — such as *Magnaporthe oryzae* (wheat and
rice blast) — as a major and growing cause of crop yield loss that threatens global food
security, urging coordinated action on fungal disease in crops.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Cryptococcosis.body:
# Cryptococcosis

Cryptococcosis is infection by encapsulated *Cryptococcus* yeasts (*C. neoformans*,
*C. gattii*), ranging from pulmonary disease to life-threatening meningitis. The review
cites cryptococcosis among major human and wildlife fungal diseases, noting *C. gattii*
infections in marine mammals as evidence of fungal threats spreading across hosts.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Cryptococcus gattii.body:
# Cryptococcus gattii

*Cryptococcus gattii* is an encapsulated yeast pathogen that causes cryptococcosis in
otherwise healthy hosts. The review cites it as an example of a fungus crossing into new
hosts and geographies: the VGIIa type infected harbor seals (*Phoca vitulina*) in British
Columbia, Canada, illustrating fungal threats to wildlife linked to environmental change.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Fluconazole.body:
# Fluconazole

Fluconazole is a triazole antifungal that inhibits fungal ergosterol biosynthesis (lanosterol
14-alpha-demethylase). The review uses azoles such as fluconazole to illustrate both the
mainstay of antifungal therapy and the worldwide emergence of antifungal resistance, which is
amplified by the parallel agricultural use of azole fungicides.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Fungal kingdom.body:
# Fungal kingdom

The fungal kingdom (Fungi) has diversified over the past billion years to more than two
million species, with over 95% still undescribed. Fungi are heterotrophs that feed on
almost any organic carbon, recycling nutrients through the decay of dead plants and animals
and sequestering carbon into Earth's ecosystems. Beyond macroscopic mushrooms and
microscopic yeast, the kingdom spans mutualists (mycorrhizae), saprotrophs and pathogens of
plants, wildlife and humans.

Estimated total species: >2,000,000 (over 95% undescribed) — reported in the review.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Fungal melanin.body:
# Fungal melanin

Melanin is a pigment produced by many fungi that contributes to stress resistance and
virulence. The review notes functions of fungal melanin beyond virulence — including that
ionizing radiation changes the electronic properties of melanin and can enhance the growth
of melanized fungi, a property with potential biotechnological applications such as
radiation shielding.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Invasive aspergillosis.body:
# Invasive aspergillosis

Invasive aspergillosis is a severe mould infection, most often caused by *Aspergillus
fumigatus*, affecting immunocompromised and critically ill patients. The review notes that
invasive mould infections surged after Hurricane Harvey and that azole resistance in
*A. fumigatus* complicates treatment, contributing to severe fungal disease incidence and
mortality.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Ionizing radiation tolerance.body:
# Ionizing radiation tolerance

Ionizing radiation tolerance is the capacity of some melanized fungi to withstand, and even
grow faster under, ionizing radiation. The review cites observations that ionizing radiation
changes the electronic properties of fungal melanin and enhances the growth of melanized
fungi, a trait of interest for biotechnological applications such as radiation-shielding
materials.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Magnaporthe oryzae.body:
# Magnaporthe oryzae

*Magnaporthe oryzae* (*Pyricularia oryzae*) is the fungus that causes rice blast and the
emerging wheat blast disease. The review cites it among crop-destroying fungi that threaten
global food security, noting the growing urgency of fungal disease in crops and its
geographic spread aided by human movement and a changing climate.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Nutrient cycling.body:
# Nutrient cycling

Nutrient cycling is the movement and recycling of nutrients through decomposition. The review
underscores fungi as principal decomposers: by feeding on dead plants and animals, fungi
recycle nutrients and sequester carbon, making them indispensable to the functioning of
terrestrial ecosystems.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Penicillin.body:
# Penicillin

Penicillin is a beta-lactam antibiotic originally derived from *Penicillium* fungi. The
review cites penicillin as a landmark example of human-directed applications of fungi:
pharmaceuticals, including antibiotics, that have transformed medicine. It exemplifies the
beneficial side of the fungal kingdom that the review proposes harnessing further for the
bioeconomy.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Pseudogymnoascus destructans.body:
# Pseudogymnoascus destructans

*Pseudogymnoascus destructans* is a psychrophilic fungus that causes white-nose syndrome in
hibernating bats, killing millions across North America. The review presents it as a
striking example of a cold-adapted fungal pathogen driving wildlife mortality, with spread
facilitated by human movement between bat hibernacula.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Psilocybin.body:
# Psilocybin

Psilocybin is a psychoactive tryptamine alkaloid produced by *Psilocybe* and related
mushrooms. The review lists psilocybin among the psychoactive compounds that exemplify
human-directed applications of fungi, alongside antibiotics and other pharmaceuticals,
highlighting the fungal kingdom as a source of bioactive natural products.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Western United States.body:
# Western United States

The arid and semi-arid western United States is the region endemic for *Coccidioides* and
coccidioidomycosis. The review cites longitudinal surveillance there showing that climate
variables (precipitation, heat, drought) drive the incidence and geographic expansion of
Valley fever.

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

Wheat blast.body:
# Wheat blast

Wheat blast is an emerging, fast-spreading crop disease caused by the *Magnaporthe oryzae*
(Triticum lineage) fungus, threatening global wheat production. The review uses it to argue
for addressing the growing urgency of fungal disease in crops, noting that fungal crop
pathogens jeopardize food security and that their range may expand with climate change.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.object: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

White-nose syndrome.body:
# White-nose syndrome

White-nose syndrome is a fungal disease of hibernating bats caused by *Pseudogymnoascus
destructans*, characterized by white fungal growth on the muzzle and wings and massive
mortality. The review presents it as an emblematic wildlife fungal epizootic that has killed
millions of bats across North America, with spread linked to human movement between caves.

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review

edges.primary_source: Case 2025 fungal impacts on Earth's ecosystems review
